npc_seeds.name: Cassandra 'Echo' Vasquez, Court Seer
npc_seeds.summary: A neonate Malkavian who serves as the Prince's unofficial prophet, speaking in cryptic riddles that often prove disturbingly accurate.
npc_seeds.temperament: intense, distracted, speaks in fragments and metaphors that hint at deeper truths
possible_plots: Echo delivers a prophecy that directly involves the coterie, but her words are so cryptic that the PCs must interpret them before disaster strikes.
She begins having visions of an Antediluvian's awakening and seeks the coterie's help to verify or prevent the coming horror.
The Prince orders Echo to read the auras of the coterie, and she reveals secrets they've been hiding—but also warns of a threat they didn't know existed.

npc_seeds.name: Marcus 'Trick' O'Malley, Chaos Agent
npc_seeds.summary: An ancilla prankster who uses elaborate schemes to expose hypocrisy and reveal uncomfortable truths in Kindred society.
npc_seeds.role: agent of revelation and court jester
npc_seeds.temperament: manic, clever, finds humor in the darkest truths and uses pranks as a form of communication
possible_plots: Trick orchestrates a prank that reveals a prominent elder's secret betrayal, forcing the coterie to decide whether to expose the truth or cover it up.
He targets the coterie with a series of escalating pranks that force them to question their own assumptions about reality and morality.
Trick's latest prank goes too far and accidentally triggers a blood hunt; the coterie must help him escape or face the consequences themselves.

npc_seeds.name: Dr. Eleanor 'Pattern' Chen, Information Broker
npc_seeds.summary: A Malkavian obsessed with collecting patterns—connections between seemingly unrelated events, people, and places. She maintains a vast network of contacts and information.
npc_seeds.temperament: methodical, obsessive, sees connections others miss but struggles with social interaction
possible_plots: Pattern approaches the coterie with information about a conspiracy, but her obsession with patterns leads her to see connections that may or may not be real.
She becomes fixated on collecting information about the coterie, following them and documenting their actions in her ever-expanding database.
Pattern's collection reveals a hidden pattern that threatens the entire city, but she needs the coterie's help to understand and act on it.

npc_seeds.name: Julian 'Harmony' Blackwood, Mad Composer
npc_seeds.summary: A Malkavian composer haunted by music from the Cobweb, creating works that carry hidden meanings and affect listeners in profound, sometimes dangerous ways.
npc_seeds.role: court musician and prophet through art
npc_seeds.temperament: distracted, half-listening to conversations while the music in his head demands attention, polite but distant
possible_plots: Harmony's latest composition contains a prophecy encoded in the music, and the coterie must decipher it before a disaster occurs.
He performs at Elysium, and his music triggers unexpected reactions in the audience—some fall into torpor, others frenzy, and a few gain sudden insights.
Harmony asks the coterie to help him track down the source of a melody he's hearing in the Cobweb, which leads them to a dangerous discovery.

npc_seeds.name: Sister Mary 'Sanctuary' O'Brien, Asylum Inmate
npc_seeds.summary: An elder Malkavian who has made a mental institution her haven, finding sanctuary and purpose among the mentally ill while using the asylum as a source of insight and feeding.
npc_seeds.temperament: calm, maternal, sees the asylum as a place of truth where madness is accepted rather than hidden
possible_plots: Sanctuary offers the coterie refuge in the asylum when they're being hunted, but her presence there creates complications with mortal authorities.
She reveals that several of the asylum's patients are actually ghouls or Kindred who have been driven mad, and asks the coterie to help them.
Sanctuary's connection to the Cobweb allows her to sense when other Malkavians are in danger, and she sends warnings through the coterie.

npc_seeds.name: Alex 'Whisper' Kim, Network Node
npc_seeds.summary: A neonate with an unusually strong connection to the Cobweb, serving as a communication hub for other Malkavians in the city.
npc_seeds.temperament: overwhelmed, constantly hearing voices from the Network, struggles to filter signal from noise
possible_plots: Whisper receives a message through the Cobweb that warns of an imminent attack, but the message is fragmented and needs the coterie's help to interpret.
The voices in Whisper's head become too much, and she asks the coterie to help her find a way to dampen the connection without losing it entirely.
Whisper accidentally broadcasts the coterie's secrets through the Network, and now every Malkavian in the city knows information they shouldn't.

npc_seeds.name: Dr. Douglas Netchurch, Methuselah's Pawn
npc_seeds.summary: An ancilla Malkavian who serves as a pawn for an ancient Methuselah, conducting experiments and gathering information while maintaining a facade of scientific objectivity.
npc_seeds.role: researcher and pawn of ancient powers
npc_seeds.temperament: cold, methodical, maintains emotional distance while being deeply connected to the Cobweb and his master's will
possible_plots: Netchurch approaches the coterie with a research proposal that would require them to submit to dangerous experiments, offering valuable information in return.
He reveals that a Methuselah is manipulating events in the city, and the coterie must decide whether to work with or against these ancient powers.
Netchurch's experiments begin to affect the Cobweb itself, causing all Malkavians in the city to experience shared visions and derangements.

npc_seeds.name: Raven 'Fever' Cross, Sabbat Lunatic
npc_seeds.summary: A Sabbat 'antitribu' Malkavian whose madness has been amplified by the sect's practices, making her a dangerous weapon of infectious insanity.
npc_seeds.temperament: unpredictable, violent, her madness is contagious and spreads to those around her
possible_plots: Fever infiltrates Camarilla territory and begins spreading her infectious madness, forcing the coterie to track her down before the entire city goes mad.
She approaches the coterie with an offer to defect from the Sabbat, but her condition makes her a liability that could expose them all.
Fever's presence in the city triggers a response from Camarilla Malkavians who remember the split at Domazlice and fear what she represents.

npc_seeds.name: Master 'Zen' Tanaka, Madness Teacher
npc_seeds.summary: An elder Malkavian who teaches other Kindred (and sometimes mortals) to see reality's true angles, using madness as a form of enlightenment.
npc_seeds.role: teacher and guide to enlightenment through madness
npc_seeds.temperament: serene, cryptic, speaks in koans and paradoxes that force listeners to question their assumptions
possible_plots: Zen offers to teach a member of the coterie to see through illusions, but the training involves embracing a form of madness that may be permanent.
He reveals that the coterie is trapped in an illusion created by a powerful enemy, and only by accepting madness can they break free.
Zen's teachings attract dangerous attention from those who fear what he represents, and the coterie must protect him or face the consequences.

npc_seeds.name: DJ 'Static' Rodriguez, Talk Radio Host
npc_seeds.summary: A Malkavian who hosts a late-night talk radio show, broadcasting insights and prophecies to mortals who don't realize they're listening to a vampire.
npc_seeds.role: broadcaster of truth and prophet to mortals
npc_seeds.temperament: charismatic, manic, uses the radio as a way to communicate truths that would be dangerous to speak directly
possible_plots: Static's radio show begins predicting events that come true, attracting dangerous attention from both mortals and Kindred who want to control or silence him.
He broadcasts information about the coterie's activities, forcing them to confront him or find a way to use his show to their advantage.
Static receives a message through the Cobweb that he feels compelled to broadcast, but doing so would violate the Masquerade and expose the Kindred.
